_comment: PT-3 (Overhaul P2): the single-source registry of cross-cutting user-facing CLAIMS. Each claim OSSICRO makes about itself that has already burned us (a stale hardcoded copy asserting something no longer true) lives HERE, once: the server injects this registry into the SPA boot payload and both surfaces render from it — no second authored copy may exist. 'tripwire' is the distinctive literal the duplication test greps app/ for: if it appears in app source outside the registry/render path, the test fails (this is the tripwire that would have caught the third hardcoded 'Still in draft.'). 'true_when' states the stage/config conditions under which the claim is true — a claim rendered outside those conditions is a bug, not copy.

claims.text: Every document on this page is a draft for qualified human review. OSSICRO never obtains consent, never approves for an IRB, never determines causality, never signs, and never submits to FDA. Each of those acts is a gate: a step owned by a named person, where the system stops and waits until that person acts.
claims.tripwire: never signs, and never submits to FDA
claims.true_when: Always — drafts-only is a Constitution hard line (HC1): no configuration, flag, or stage makes OSSICRO sign, send, or submit.

claims.text: Nothing has been submitted to the FDA, and no decision has been made.
claims.tripwire: has been submitted to the FDA
claims.true_when: Case stage draft, committed, or released. NOT rendered at the enrolled stage — treatment has begun, so the enrolled patient notice makes no submission claim (MAJOR-2).
surfaces: patient status page notice (server STRINGS patient_notice)

claims.text: Names, dates, geography, identifiers, and free text never leave this machine.
claims.true_when: Registry matching sends only de-identified predicates (codes, age band, sex, coded route) through the audited egress gateway with live egress DISABLED, and the live concept reviewer is off (OSSICRO_LIVE_CONCEPT_REVIEW unset selects the offline stub). The tie shipped in Overhaul P3: the SPA appends the server-composed live-reviewer disclosure (ai-review-live) wherever this claim renders whenever the check reports a live reviewer, so it never renders unqualified while document text can leave the machine.

claims.text: No direct patient identifiers appear in this view — the patient is shown by coded ID only.
claims.true_when: Manufacturer inbox: the release-time snapshot carries the coded id, drug, indication, and LOA-request text only; nothing else is released into the view.

claims.text: Reviewed by the offline built-in checker — nothing left this machine.
claims.true_when: The deterministic stub concept reviewer ran (OSSICRO_LIVE_CONCEPT_REVIEW unset or non-affirmative, no API key, or the live reviewer failed to construct and fell back). The review happened in-process; no document text egressed, so no ai_review audit record is written — no egress happened (B1).

claims.text: These drafts were reviewed by an external AI model ({model}) — document text left this machine under the deployment's signed BAA.
claims.true_when: OSSICRO_LIVE_CONCEPT_REVIEW is affirmatively set AND an API key is present AND the live Claude reviewer actually constructed (a fallback to the stub renders the offline claim instead). {model} is interpolated by the server from the reviewer's model_id at render time. Turning the flag on is a deployment decision with preconditions — a signed BAA or documented de-identified projection, zero-retention configuration, and the named human recorded in the deployment log; see docs/deployment/AI-REVIEW-PRECONDITIONS.md (B1 full remediation, Overhaul P3). Every live review writes one ai_review audit record (HC5).
